economic_notes: Subsequent to year-end (April 1, 2023), entities were restructured. First Nation held 100% of Serpent River First Nation Economic Development Corporation (which held Agawa Crafts and Canadian Carver). Post-restructuring includes Serpent River First Nation Limited Partnership holding Agawa Corporation and Agawa Limited Partnership. Robinson Huron Treaty $10 billion settlement approved November 1, 2023.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to inability to verify wage expenses from employee hours worked. Also qualified regarding PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations adopted April 1, 2022 - management did not complete assessment of tangible capital assets for potential asset retirement obligations. Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: Serpent River First Nation Economic Development Corporation and Serpent River First Nation Non-Profit Housing. No budget prepared for the year. current_liabilities estimated as accounts payable $814,498 plus due to funders $467,538. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $11,066,149 + Gov of Canada $317,111 + Gov of Canada Trust Funds $5,637 + Province of Ontario $2,551,101 + North Shore Tribal Council $384,039 + OFNLP $805,112 + Union of Ontario Indians $497021 = $15,626,170 (note: Health Canada $49,162 and CMHC $260,646 recognized per deferred revenue note but not shown as separate line on income statement). OSR includes sales revenue, interest, rents, and recovery of prior years.
